Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1351,7 +1351,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-rppi","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-rs2r","title":"FTUI-07.2 Add snapshot/golden suite for migrated views across terminal sizes","description":"## Background\nVisual regressions are common during screen migration and must be gateable.\n\n## Deliverables\n- snapshot suite for all migrated views\n- matrix of terminal dimensions and key states\n- update/review workflow for snapshot changes\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- snapshots exist for all target screens\n- CI can detect and report visual drift.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:42.902120430Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.185686Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:45:48.673707587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"wa-rsaf","title":"[EPIC] Session State Persistence — Safe Mux Server Restarts","description":"## Goal
Enable safe WezTerm mux server restarts by snapshotting and restoring complete session state — layout, scrollback, process info, and agent session metadata — so that 50+ AI agent sessions can survive a mux server restart with minimal disruption.

## Background & Motivation
The WezTerm mux server (wezterm-mux-server) accumulates memory leaks over time — we observed 76GB RSS after 23 days of running 50+ AI agent panes on a 256GB RAM server. The only fix is restarting the mux server, but this kills ALL agent sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) running inside those panes.

This epic adds a "snapshot-restart-restore" workflow to wa (WezTerm Automata) so we can:
1. Capture complete mux state to SQLite before restart
2. Restart the mux server (clearing leaked memory)
3. Restore the visual layout, scrollback history, and re-launch agent processes

This is a wa-native solution (not a WezTerm fork change) that leverages wa's existing vendored mux client, SQLite storage, and async runtime.

## Architecture Overview
```
wa snapshot save → MuxSnapshot → SQLite
                     ├── PaneNode tree (layout)
                     ├── ScrollbackData (per-pane content)
                     ├── ProcessInfo (shell/agent commands)
                     └── AgentSessionRef (wa session metadata)

wa snapshot restore → Read SQLite → Reconstruct
                       ├── Create windows/tabs/splits
                       ├── Inject scrollback content
                       └── Re-launch processes
```

## Sub-beads
1. SnapshotEngine orchestrator (coordinates capture)
2. Layout restoration engine (recreates window/tab/split topology)
3. Scrollback injection engine (restores visual content)
4. Process re-launch engine (restarts shells and agents)
5. CLI commands (wa snapshot save/restore/list/diff)
6. Safe-restart workflow (atomic snapshot-restart-restore cycle)
7. E2E tests (roundtrip verification)
8. Documentation (user guide and architecture)

## Dependencies
Requires: bd-cuz (schema), bd-nz6 (SQLite tables), bd-ybq (scrollback capture), bd-2t2 (layout capture)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa snapshot save` captures full mux state in <5 seconds for 50 panes
- `wa snapshot restore` recreates layout with correct split topology
- Scrollback content visible in restored panes
- Agent processes re-launched with correct working directories
- Safe-restart workflow is atomic (rollback on failure)
- E2E tests pass with roundtrip verification","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:30:52.071379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:32:07.086281Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

wa snapshot restore → Read SQLite → Reconstruct

 {"id":"wa-rsaf.1","title":"Session persistence unit test suite — 60+ tests, mocks, fixtures, structured logging","description":"## Goal
Comprehensive unit test suite for every session persistence component, designed to run WITHOUT a real WezTerm mux server, using mocks and fixtures for fast CI execution and thorough coverage.

## Background & Motivation
The session persistence system (wa-rsaf epic) has 12 beads spanning schema, storage, capture, orchestration, restoration, and CLI. Each component needs isolated unit tests that verify correctness independently, long before the full E2E roundtrip can be tested. This prevents "works in my head" bugs and ensures each piece is solid before integration.

Unit tests catch bugs that E2E tests miss: serialization edge cases, SQL migration correctness, tree reconstruction corner cases, content-hash collisions, and encoding issues. They also serve as living documentation for the data formats and algorithms.

## Test Modules & Cases

### 1. Schema Serialization (tests bd-cuz)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/schema_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod schema_tests {
    // Round-trip: MuxSnapshot -> JSON -> MuxSnapshot (verify all fields survive)
    #[test] fn mux_snapshot_roundtrip_json()
    // Round-trip: PaneNode tree -> JSON -> PaneNode tree (nested splits)
    #[test] fn pane_node_tree_roundtrip_deep_nesting()
    // Edge: empty snapshot (0 windows, 0 panes)
    #[test] fn empty_snapshot_serializes()
    // Edge: PaneNode with 5 levels of nested splits
    #[test] fn deeply_nested_split_topology()
    // Edge: ProcessInfo with None argv, None pid
    #[test] fn process_info_optional_fields()
    // Edge: Unicode in titles, CWD paths with spaces/emoji
    #[test] fn unicode_in_all_string_fields()
    // Edge: Very large pane_id values (u64::MAX)
    #[test] fn extreme_pane_id_values()
    // Verify: PaneDimensions captures rows, cols, scrollback_rows correctly
    #[test] fn pane_dimensions_capture()
    // Verify: AgentSessionRef links correctly
    #[test] fn agent_session_ref_links()
    // Verify: snapshot_id is valid UUID format
    #[test] fn snapshot_id_is_valid_uuid()
}
```

**Logging**: Each test logs with `tracing::info!` at entry/exit. Failed assertions include the actual vs expected JSON diff.

### 2. SQLite Storage (tests bd-nz6)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/storage_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod storage_tests {
    // Schema migration: v20 -> v21 creates tables correctly
    #[test] fn schema_migration_creates_snapshot_tables()
    // Insert + query: store snapshot, retrieve by ID
    #[test] fn insert_and_retrieve_snapshot()
    // Scrollback dedup: same content_hash stored once
    #[test] fn scrollback_dedup_same_hash()
    // Scrollback dedup: different content_hash stored separately
    #[test] fn scrollback_dedup_different_hash()
    // Retention: keep last N, delete older
    #[test] fn retention_cleanup_keeps_last_n()
    // Retention: keep all from last 24h regardless of count
    #[test] fn retention_keeps_recent_snapshots()
    // Checksum: verify SHA-256 integrity on read
    #[test] fn checksum_integrity_verification()
    // Edge: very large snapshot_json (1MB+)
    #[test] fn large_snapshot_json_storage()
    // Edge: concurrent reads during retention cleanup
    #[test] fn concurrent_read_during_cleanup()
    // Verify: indexes exist for snapshot_id, created_at, content_hash
    #[test] fn required_indexes_exist()
}
```

**Infrastructure**: Uses `rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory()` for every test. No disk I/O.

### 3. Layout Tree Reconstruction (tests bd-2t2)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/layout_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod layout_tests {
    // Single pane (no splits) -> PaneNode::Leaf
    #[test] fn single_pane_layout()
    // Two panes horizontal split -> HSplit
    #[test] fn two_pane_horizontal_split()
    // Two panes vertical split -> VSplit
    #[test] fn two_pane_vertical_split()
    // Three panes: one left, two stacked right
    #[test] fn three_pane_l_shape()
    // Four panes: 2x2 grid
    #[test] fn four_pane_grid()
    // Deep nesting: 5 levels of alternating H/V splits
    #[test] fn deeply_nested_alternating_splits()
    // Split ratios: verify 70/30, 50/50, 25/75 captured correctly
    #[test] fn split_ratio_accuracy()
    // Active pane tracking: correct pane marked active
    #[test] fn active_pane_correct()
    // Multiple tabs: each tab has independent layout
    #[test] fn multi_tab_independent_layouts()
    // Workspaces: windows grouped by workspace
    #[test] fn workspace_grouping()
    // Edge: panes with zero-size dimensions (collapsed splits)
    #[test] fn collapsed_split_zero_size()
    // Edge: single pane after all splits closed
    #[test] fn single_pane_after_split_close()
}
```

**Fixtures**: Pre-recorded `wezterm cli list` JSON output stored as `fixtures/layout/*.json`. Each fixture has a corresponding expected `PaneNode` tree in `fixtures/layout/*.expected.json`.

### 4. Scrollback Capture & Dedup (tests bd-ybq)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/scrollback_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod scrollback_tests {
    // Content hash: same content produces same hash
    #[test] fn content_hash_deterministic()
    // Content hash: different content produces different hash
    #[test] fn content_hash_unique()
    // Chunked capture: large scrollback fetched in 1000-line chunks
    #[test] fn chunked_capture_reassembly()
    // Dedup: unchanged scrollback returns same hash, skips re-capture
    #[test] fn unchanged_scrollback_dedup()
    // ANSI preservation: colors, bold, underline survive roundtrip
    #[test] fn ansi_attributes_preserved()
    // Wide chars: CJK characters with proper column counting
    #[test] fn wide_character_handling()
    // Hyperlinks: OSC 8 links survive capture
    #[test] fn hyperlink_preservation()
    // Alt-screen: primary screen captured, alt-screen flagged
    #[test] fn alt_screen_handling()
    // Truncation: >100K lines truncated from top, recent kept
    #[test] fn large_scrollback_truncation()
    // Edge: empty scrollback (new pane, no output yet)
    #[test] fn empty_scrollback()
    // Edge: binary content safely escaped
    #[test] fn binary_content_escaping()
    // Performance: capture 50 panes x 5000 lines < 5s (using mock)
    #[test] fn capture_performance_benchmark()
}
```

### 5. Restoration Engines (tests wa-e6pq, wa-1xcz, wa-32z7)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/restore_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod restore_tests {
    // Layout: single pane restored correctly
    #[test] fn restore_single_pane_layout()
    // Layout: complex split tree restored with correct ratios
    #[test] fn restore_complex_split_tree()
    // Layout: PaneIdMap maps old IDs to new IDs correctly
    #[test] fn pane_id_mapping_correctness()
    // Scrollback injection: ANSI content injected to correct pane
    #[test] fn scrollback_injection_content()
    // Scrollback injection: chunked writes for large content
    #[test] fn scrollback_injection_chunking()
    // Scrollback injection: ANSI reset prefix prevents state contamination
    #[test] fn scrollback_injection_reset_prefix()
    // Process launch plan: shell -> LaunchShell
    #[test] fn process_plan_shell()
    // Process launch plan: claude -> LaunchAgent (when opt-in)
    #[test] fn process_plan_agent_optin()
    // Process launch plan: unknown -> Manual with hint
    #[test] fn process_plan_unknown_manual()
    // Process launch plan: --dry-run shows plan without executing
    #[test] fn process_plan_dry_run()
    // Edge: pane that failed layout restore is skipped in scrollback injection
    #[test] fn failed_pane_skipped_gracefully()
    // Edge: staggered launches respect delay
    #[test] fn staggered_launch_timing()
}
```

### 6. SnapshotEngine Orchestrator (tests wa-29k1)
**File**: `crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/engine_tests.rs`

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod engine_tests {
    // Full capture: mock mux with 3 panes, verify all components called
    #[test] fn full_capture_all_components()
    // Partial failure: one pane fails, others succeed
    #[test] fn partial_failure_continues()
    // Concurrent capture: 10 panes captured concurrently
    #[test] fn concurrent_capture_10_panes()
    // Periodic trigger: no-change detection skips capture
    #[test] fn periodic_no_change_skip()
    // Periodic trigger: changed pane triggers capture
    #[test] fn periodic_changed_pane_captures()
    // Snapshot metadata: version, timestamp, pane count correct
    #[test] fn snapshot_metadata_correct()
}
```

## Logging Strategy
Every test module uses `tracing_subscriber` initialized with `tracing::Level::DEBUG`:
```rust
fn init_test_logging() {
    let _ = tracing_subscriber::fmt()
        .with_test_writer()  // Capture in test output
        .with_max_level(tracing::Level::DEBUG)
        .with_target(true)
        .with_file(true)
        .with_line_number(true)
        .try_init();
}
```

Test output includes:
- Component under test
- Input fixture name
- Expected vs actual values on failure (JSON diff)
- Timing for performance-sensitive tests
- Structured spans for multi-step operations

## Mock Infrastructure
```rust
// crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/test_helpers.rs
pub struct MockMuxClient { /* mock DirectMuxClient with configurable responses */ }
pub fn fixture_snapshot(name: &str) -> MuxSnapshot { /* load from fixtures/ */ }
pub fn fixture_layout(name: &str) -> PaneNode { /* load from fixtures/ */ }
pub fn fixture_scrollback(lines: usize) -> ScrollbackData { /* generate test content */ }
```

## Dependencies
- bd-cuz (schema must be designed first — tests verify the schema)
- bd-nz6 (SQLite tables must be designed first — tests verify the tables)
- Should be implemented incrementally: schema tests first, then storage tests, then capture tests, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 60+ unit tests across 6 modules
- All tests pass with `cargo test snapshot` 
- Zero dependency on running WezTerm instance
- Tests complete in < 10 seconds total
- Mock infrastructure reusable for future tests
- Every test has structured logging output visible with `--nocapture`
- Fixture files committed to repo","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:03:06.060784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:30:23.948433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:30:23.948433Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.1","depends_on_id":"wa-3q7q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:04:27.372757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.1","depends_on_id":"wa-cuz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:04:27.265014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:04:27.372757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.1","depends_on_id":"wa-rbvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:04:27.265014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.1","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:03:06.060784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-rsaf.2","title":"Formal session restoration verification via proptest","description":"## Goal
Use property-based testing (proptest) to formally verify session restoration correctness: for all valid mux states S, restore(snapshot(S)) is approximately equal to S (isomorphic up to PIDs and timestamps).

## Background and Motivation
Session persistence (wa-rsaf) is the most critical feature -- if restore produces incorrect state, users lose work. Traditional unit tests check specific cases but cannot cover the full state space. Property-based testing generates thousands of random valid states and verifies the isomorphism property holds for ALL of them.

## Technical Design

### Isomorphism Specification
Define approximately equal for mux states:
```rust
fn is_isomorphic(original: &MuxSnapshot, restored: &MuxSnapshot) -> bool {
    // Layout topology must be identical
    original.layout_tree.is_structurally_equal(&restored.layout_tree)
    // Pane count must match
    && original.panes.len() == restored.panes.len()
    // Scrollback content must match (up to trailing whitespace)
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.scrollback_trimmed() == b.scrollback_trimmed())
    // Cursor positions must match
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.cursor == b.cursor)
    // Working directories must match
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.cwd == b.cwd)
    // PIDs may differ (new processes)
    // Timestamps may differ (restored at different time)
}
```

### Property-Based Strategy
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn snapshot_restore_roundtrip(state in arb_mux_state()) {
        let snapshot = capture_snapshot(&state)?;
        let restored = restore_snapshot(&snapshot)?;
        prop_assert!(is_isomorphic(&state, &restored));
    }
}
```

### Arbitrary State Generation
Generate valid mux states with configurable complexity:
- 1-20 windows, 1-10 tabs per window, 1-5 panes per tab
- Random split directions (horizontal/vertical) with valid ratios
- Scrollback: 0-10000 lines of random text with ANSI escapes
- Cursor positions within valid bounds
- Working directories from a pool of valid paths
- Agent types from valid set

### Edge Case Properties
```rust
// Empty state roundtrips
prop_assert!(is_isomorphic(&empty_state(), &restore(snapshot(empty_state()))));

// Single pane roundtrips
proptest!(|s in arb_single_pane_state()| ...);

// Maximum complexity roundtrips
proptest!(|s in arb_max_complexity_state()| ...);

// Mid-write snapshot (concurrent modification)
proptest!(|s in arb_state_with_pending_writes()| ...);

// Partial failure recovery
proptest!(|s in arb_state(), fail_point in arb_fail_point()| {
    // Inject failure during restore, verify partial state is consistent
});
```

### Implementation Location
- New: crates/wa-core/tests/proptest_snapshot.rs
- Depends on: wa-rsaf snapshot infrastructure

## Existing Code References
- wa-rsaf beads: MuxSnapshot schema, SnapshotEngine, layout restoration, scrollback injection
- proptest crate: strategy generation framework

## Dependencies
- Depends on wa-rsaf session persistence (needs snapshot/restore to be implemented first)
- Should be created alongside or immediately after wa-rsaf implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- proptest strategies for arbitrary valid mux states
- Roundtrip property: restore(snapshot(S)) approximately equals S verified for 10000+ random states
- Edge cases: empty, single-pane, max-complexity, mid-write, partial-failure
- Isomorphism specification documented and implemented
- Regression: any snapshot/restore change must pass all proptest cases
- Run time: < 60 seconds for full proptest suite

## Specific Proptest Strategies

### Arbitrary Layout Trees
```rust
/// Generate arbitrary valid layout trees with controlled complexity.
fn arb_layout_tree() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
    let leaf = (arb_pane_id(), arb_cwd(), arb_pane_size())
        .prop_map(|(id, cwd, size)| PaneNode::Leaf {
            pane_id: id, cwd, size,
        });

    // Recursive: split nodes with 2 children, up to 6 levels deep
    leaf.prop_recursive(
        6,    // max depth
        50,   // max nodes
        2,    // items per collection (binary splits)
        |inner| {
            (arb_split_direction(), inner.clone(), inner, arb_ratio())
                .prop_map(|(dir, left, right, ratio)| PaneNode::Split {
                    direction: dir,
                    children: vec![left, right],
                    ratio,
                })
        },
    )
}

fn arb_pane_size() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneSize> {
    (1u16..=500, 1u16..=200)
        .prop_map(|(cols, rows)| PaneSize { cols, rows })
}

fn arb_split_direction() -> impl Strategy<Value = SplitDirection> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(SplitDirection::Horizontal),
        Just(SplitDirection::Vertical),
    ]
}

fn arb_ratio() -> impl Strategy<Value = f64> {
    (10u32..=90).prop_map(|r| r as f64 / 100.0)
}
```

### Random Scrollback Content
```rust
/// Generate random scrollback with realistic terminal output characteristics.
fn arb_scrollback_content() -> impl Strategy<Value = Vec<ScrollbackLine>> {
    prop::collection::vec(arb_scrollback_line(), 0..10000)
}

fn arb_scrollback_line() -> impl Strategy<Value = ScrollbackLine> {
    prop_oneof![
        // Plain text lines (most common)
        60 => "[a-zA-Z0-9 .,:;!?-]{0,200}"
            .prop_map(|text| ScrollbackLine::plain(text)),
        // Lines with ANSI color attributes
        20 => (arb_sgr_params(), ".{0,150}")
            .prop_map(|(sgr, text)| ScrollbackLine::attributed(text, sgr)),
        // Lines with wide characters (CJK)
        10 => prop::collection::vec(arb_cjk_char(), 0..80)
            .prop_map(|chars| ScrollbackLine::plain(
                chars.into_iter().collect::<String>()
            )),
        // Lines with hyperlinks (OSC 8)
        5 => (arb_url(), ".{1,50}")
            .prop_map(|(url, label)| ScrollbackLine::hyperlink(url, label)),
        // Empty lines
        5 => Just(ScrollbackLine::plain(String::new())),
    ]
}

fn arb_sgr_params() -> impl Strategy<Value = Vec<SgrParam>> {
    prop::collection::vec(
        prop_oneof![
            Just(SgrParam::Bold),
            Just(SgrParam::Italic),
            Just(SgrParam::Underline),
            (0u8..=255).prop_map(SgrParam::FgColor256),
            (0u8..=255).prop_map(SgrParam::BgColor256),
            (0u8..=255, 0u8..=255, 0u8..=255)
                .prop_map(|(r, g, b)| SgrParam::FgColorRgb(r, g, b)),
        ],
        0..5,
    )
}
```

### Random Process Configurations
```rust
/// Generate random process configurations for snapshot restoration testing.
fn arb_process_config() -> impl Strategy<Value = ProcessInfo> {
    prop_oneof![
        // Shell processes (most common)
        60 => (arb_shell_type(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(shell, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![shell],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Shell,
            }),
        // Agent processes
        20 => (arb_agent_type(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(agent, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![agent.binary_name()],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Agent(agent),
            }),
        // Interactive programs (vim, htop, etc.)
        10 => (arb_interactive_program(), arb_cwd())
            .prop_map(|(prog, cwd)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![prog],
                cwd,
                env: HashMap::new(),
                process_type: ProcessType::Interactive,
            }),
        // Unknown/custom processes
        10 => (arb_command_path(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(cmd, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![cmd],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Unknown,
            }),
    ]
}

fn arb_shell_type() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just("bash".to_string()),
        Just("zsh".to_string()),
        Just("fish".to_string()),
        Just("/bin/sh".to_string()),
    ]
}

fn arb_agent_type() -> impl Strategy<Value = AgentType> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(AgentType::ClaudeCode),
        Just(AgentType::Codex),
        Just(AgentType::GeminiCli),
    ]
}

fn arb_cwd() -> impl Strategy<Value = PathBuf> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user/project")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/tmp")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/var/log")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user/projects/wezterm_automata")),
    ]
}

fn arb_env_vars() -> impl Strategy<Value = HashMap<String, String>> {
    prop::collection::hash_map("[A-Z_]{1,20}", ".{0,100}", 0..10)
}
```

## Criterion Benchmarks for Property Test Throughput
Add benchmarks in crates/wa-core/benches/proptest_throughput.rs:
- bench_arb_mux_state_generation: Measure generation throughput of arb_mux_state(), target >1000 states/s
- bench_isomorphism_check: Measure is_isomorphic() comparison for two 50-pane snapshots, target <1ms
- bench_roundtrip_single_iteration: Single capture-restore-verify cycle for a 20-pane state, target <50ms (enabling 10000 iterations in <60s budget)
- bench_arb_scrollback_generation: Measure generation throughput of 10000-line random scrollback, target >100/s

## Cross-References
- **wa-rsaf.1** (Unit tests): Unit tests in wa-rsaf.1 cover specific known-good and known-bad cases (e.g., restore a 3-tab layout with known split ratios). The proptest suite in this bead (wa-rsaf.2) complements unit tests by exploring the unknown state space -- finding edge cases that manual test design would miss.
